title: A Democracy Safe for the World
gist: In the readable opening of this otherwise paywalled review essay, Berman frames the deepening crisis of established democracies, noting that US institutional dysfunction has become so severe that V-Dem judges the country to be backsliding faster than any other democracy in modern times.

insights.claim: Across long-established democracies once thought stable, polarization has risen, trust in political institutions has fallen, and governments appear unable to respond effectively to their countries' challenges.
insights.speaker: Sheri Berman
insights.so_what: Eroding institutional trust raises political and policy uncertainty across developed markets, a slow-burn risk factor.
insights.quote: polarization has risen, trust in political institutions has fallen
insights.locator: Intro / 'Over the past decade or two'

insights.claim: US institutional dysfunction has become so severe over the past year that the country is backsliding 'much faster' than any other democracy in modern times, according to the Varieties of Democracy think tank.
insights.speaker: Sheri Berman
insights.so_what: An explicit measure of accelerating US democratic decline, relevant to governance-risk and institutional-stability assessments.
insights.quote: the country is backsliding “much faster” than “any other democracy in modern times”
insights.locator: Intro / 'In the United States, internal divisions'
